title: buildah rmi tagged images also remove the original image
body: After `buildah tag`, the tagged image ID is same with the original image ID, so remove the tagged image also remove the original image, which is not reasonable.

comments.body: Can you elaborate on this?  I'm not sure exactly what you're doing that is triggering the behavior you're seeing.

In below testing, remove `busybox2`, `busybox` got removed also. 

```
[root@fedora buildah]# ./buildah  images
IMAGE ID     IMAGE NAME
b812babf7f65 docker.io/library/redis:latest
b812babf7f65 docker.io/gouyang/redis:latest
b812babf7f65 docker.io/testing/redis:latest
b812babf7f65 docker.io/foo/redis:latest
5965adf86a6e docker.io/library/nginx:latest
223c6ba7409d docker.io/kubernetes/pause:latest
e236280e3ef7 docker.io/library/redis:alpine
a9a3ca121508 registry.access.redhat.com/rhel7/rhel:latest
8dc56e5fe235 docker.io/library/busybox:latest
8e1b8fedede7 docker.io/library/fedora:latest

[root@fedora buildah]# ./buildah tag docker.io/library/busybox docker.io/library/busybox2
           
[root@fedora buildah]# ./buildah rmi docker.io/library/busybox2
docker.io/library/busybox2

[root@fedora buildah]# ./buildah images
IMAGE ID     IMAGE NAME
b812babf7f65 docker.io/library/redis:latest
b812babf7f65 docker.io/gouyang/redis:latest
b812babf7f65 docker.io/testing/redis:latest
b812babf7f65 docker.io/foo/redis:latest
5965adf86a6e docker.io/library/nginx:latest
223c6ba7409d docker.io/kubernetes/pause:latest
e236280e3ef7 docker.io/library/redis:alpine
a9a3ca121508 registry.access.redhat.com/rhel7/rhel:latest
8e1b8fedede7 docker.io/library/fedora:latest
```

Docker just untagged the image.

```
[root@fedora buildah]# docker images
REPOSITORY          TAG                 IMAGE ID            CREATED             SIZE
docker.io/redis     latest              e32ef7250bc1        6 weeks ago         183.6 MB
docker.io/busybox   latest              00f017a8c2a6        3 months ago        1.11 MB
docker.io/redis     alpine              83638a6d3af2        3 months ago        19.82 MB
[root@fedora buildah]# docker tag docker.io/busybox docker.io/busybox2
[root@fedora buildah]# docker rmi docker.io/busybox2
Untagged: docker.io/busybox2:latest
[root@fedora buildah]# docker images
REPOSITORY          TAG                 IMAGE ID            CREATED             SIZE
docker.io/redis     latest              e32ef7250bc1        6 weeks ago         183.6 MB
docker.io/busybox   latest              00f017a8c2a6        3 months ago        1.11 MB
docker.io/redis     alpine              83638a6d3af2        3 months ago        19.82 MB
```
Ah, I see.  We're not (currently, at least) doing things the prune/no-prune way, where "rmi" would remove a tag from an image, and only remove the image itself if after doing so, it had no tags.
Am I correct in saying that untagging an image is simply removing the name from the image?

Tested with the latest code, the issue is fixed.
A minor problem is how about add a new line break to the output.

```
# ./buildah tag busybox busybox2
[root@fedora buildah]# ./buildah rmi busybox2
untagged: docker.io/library/busybox2:latest[root@fedora buildah]#
```
